summary.headline: ADM reports third quarter 2025 results with adjusted EPS of $0.92, down 16% year-over-year.
summary.narrative_summary: ADM announced its third quarter 2025 financial results, reporting adjusted net earnings of $448 million and adjusted EPS of $0.92, down 16% from the prior year. The company's total segment operating profit was $845 million, down 19% year-over-year. The decline was attributed to lower margins in crushing and carbohydrate solutions, impacted by muted demand due to deferral of U.S. biofuel policy and international trade challenges. The company revised its full-year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance to $3.25 - $3.50 per share from approximately $4.00 per share.

drivers: higher export activity in North America
concerns: lower crushing margins due to muted demand from deferral of U.S. biofuel policy

drivers: industry inventory dropping due to plant downtime
concerns: lower global demand for starches and sweeteners
persisting higher corn costs in EMEA

drivers: record revenue in Flavors North America

extraction_notes: Numbers from Exhibit 99.1; cover sheet only had Item references. Guidance changes are based on the press release text.
